title: pericopsis angolensis f. intermedia
intro: Pericopsis angolensis f. intermedia, also known as the medium or intermediate form of the African white ebony, is a tree species native to the tropical regions of central and western Africa. It has been found to have medicinal properties, particularly in its bark, which is used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The health benefits of Pericopsis angolensis f. intermedia include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help to reduce pain, fight infections, and promote overall well-being. In traditional medicine, the bark is often prepared as a decoction or infusion for its medicinal properties, and it can also be used in herbal preparations such as tinctures and ointments.
